Finish Any Game

Available challenges
FINISH

Finish as White

Complete and submit one finished game where you played as White.

This is the smallest step up from any finished game, keeping the focus on side awareness instead of winning, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

90 pts

FINISH
BLACK
Finish as Black

Complete and submit one finished game where you played as Black.

This closes the side-pair for finished-game verification without adding any new game outcome requirement, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

95 pts
Open challenge
WIN
WHITE
Win as White

Win one complete game as White.

Use this as a starter check: a simple tactical win with your first real game, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

Open challenge
WIN
BLACK
Win as Black

Win one complete game as Black.

This closes the win-side pair with the same narrow verifier loop already live for White, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

120 pts
Open challenge
DRAW
EITHER
Draw Any Game

Finish and submit one drawn game where your saved chess account appears.

This is the smallest outcome expansion after the existing finish and win checks, without adding side-specific logic, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

110 pts
Open challenge
DRAW
WHITE
Draw as White

Finish and submit one drawn game where you played as White.

This adds one side-specific draw check without widening the loop beyond the current single-game verifier path, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

115 pts
Open challenge
DRAW
BLACK
Draw as Black

Finish and submit one drawn game where you played as Black.

This closes the side-pair for draw verification using the same single-game verifier path already in production, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

120 pts
Open challenge
LOSE
EITHER
Lose Any Game

Finish and submit one lost game where your saved chess account appears.

This adds one narrow loss check using the same finished-game, identity, and decisive-result evidence already in the verifier path, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

125 pts
Open challenge
LOSE
WHITE
Lose as White

Finish and submit one lost game where you played as White.

This adds one side-specific loss check on top of the existing loss verifier without widening the single-game review loop, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.

This closes the side-pair for loss verification using the same single-game verifier path already reused across the current catalog, and it now supports both Lichess and Chess.com.
